scope: Attribute the producer-side latency in the forward-carried sidecar sparse-MLA dKV path by removing contribution-stage q/grad_output global reloads

mode2_score_qk_dov_prob_dp_checksum.read: Normal K16 query-major QK, dO.V, softmax/prob/scaled-dP page producer, checksum only.

mode15_forward_sidecar_contribution_chec.read: Uses forward-carried group_ids sidecar, then runs the normal contribution consumer with real q and grad_output reloads from global memory.

mode19_forward_sidecar_const_qgo_contrib.read: Same as mode15 through sidecar preload/use and score/prob/scaled-dP, but replaces contribution-stage q and grad_output global reloads with BF16 constants. This is an attribution-only checksum path, not a numerically meaningful dKV kernel.

mode20_forward_sidecar_const_all_contrib.read: Same as mode19, but also replaces contribution-stage prob/scaled-dP shared-memory reads with BF16 constants. It still computes and stores the score/prob/scaled-dP page, then runs the same contribution MFMA and H-wave reduction structure. This is an attribution-only checksum path, not a numerically meaningful dKV kernel.

mode16_forward_sidecar_valid_dkv.read: Same as mode15, plus valid final compressed dKV atomics.

traffic_model.read: The measured sidecar metadata path is tiny and within noise. Mode19 shows the producer wall is dominated by the contribution consumer's q/grad_output replay. Mode20 then splits the residual and shows prob/scaled-dP shared replay is the next meaningful cost, while constant-operand MFMA/reduction bookkeeping is small.

decision.read: Mode19 reduces the sidecar checksum path from 21.47 ms to 11.72 ms by removing only contribution-stage q and grad_output global reloads, attributing about 9.76 ms to real q/dO replay. Mode20 then reduces 11.72 ms to 7.57 ms by also removing contribution-stage prob/scaled-dP shared-memory reads, attributing about 4.14 ms to that shared replay. The residual constant-operand contribution MFMA/reduction structure is only about 0.50 ms over the mode-2 score/prob producer. The final atomics add about 1.48 ms in the comparable valid-dKV path. Therefore the latency added inside the sidecar producer is not the sidecar metadata; it is memory replay inside the contribution consumer.
decision.next: Keep the forward sidecar contract, but rewrite the producer so q, grad_output, prob, and scaled-dP are consumed while resident instead of replayed from global/shared memory across contribution D tiles. The dKV epilogue should then do group-aware reduction from that resident tile before the final global atomic stream.
